response:
The task is to determine the speed of the sailboat when the instantaneous wind power reaches its maximum value. The formula for wind power is given as $ F = \frac{B S \rho (v_0 - v)^2}{2} $. The maximum wind power occurs when the derivative of $ F $ with respect to $ v $ is zero. The sail area $ S $ is 7 m², the wind speed $ v_0 $ is 6.3 m/s, and $ B $ is the aerodynamic force coefficient. The goal is to find $ v(t') $ when $ N(t') = N_{\text{max}} $.

The aerodynamic force coefficient $ B $ is a constant for this scenario. The wind power is maximized when the term $ (v_0 - v)^2 $ is maximized. This occurs when $ v $ is as close as possible to $ v_0 $, but not equal to it. Therefore, the maximum wind power occurs when the sailboat is moving at the speed of the wind, $ v = v_0 $.

The speed of the sailboat at the moment when the wind power reaches its maximum value is $ v = 6.3 \, \text{m/s} $.

The speed of the sailboat at the moment when the wind power reaches its maximum value is $ \boxed{6.3} \, \text{m/s} $.
